discrepancy: PDF verbatim: '4  Associated Persons Data' (plain text heading level 1). MD verbatim: '# SDTM v2.0 — Chapter 4: Associated Persons Data' (markdown H1 with full chapter title prefix). Same semantic referent but MD adds 'SDTM v2.0 — Chapter' prefix not present in PDF.

PDF verbatim sentence is reproduced verbatim as the first sentence inside md_model_04_a004, which is a PARAGRAPH_INVALID_AGENT_ERROR merging three PDF sentences into one atom. No semantic difference for this sentence.
PDF verbatim sentence is reproduced verbatim as the second sentence inside md_model_04_a004 (merged paragraph atom). No semantic difference for this sentence.

PDF verbatim ends '...with the application of specific AP rules, including:'. MD verbatim ends '...with the application of specific AP rules:' (drops the word 'including' and the colon structure differs slightly). Core semantic content preserved.

PDF verbatim: 'Implementers creating AP domains will follow the AP assumptions for the Identifier variables.' No MD atom contains this statement. md_model_04_a006 covers prefix convention; md_model_04_a007 covers APID identifier role; neither mentions following AP assumptions for Identifier variables.

PDF verbatim: 'AP will be the prefix for the domain and dataset name, and will identify the dataset as AP data.' MD verbatim: '**Prefix convention:** AP domains use the domain code prefix `AP` followed by the relevant domain code. For example, associated persons demographics would use `APDM`.' MD adds APDM example not in PDF source verbatim, but core prefix rule is preserved.

PDF verbatim: 'The Associated Persons Identifier (APID) will be required in all AP datasets, and will identify records as AP data.' No MD atom states APID is required in all AP datasets or that it identifies records as AP data. md_model_04_a007 discusses APID being removed from Section 3.1.4 and still listed in Section 3 — different content. Overlap estimated <0.50.

PDF verbatim includes the full URL 'https://www.cdisc.org/standards/foundational/sdtmig/sdtmig-ap' and specifies 'provides implementation rules, advice, and examples'. MD verbatim: 'Additional guidance for implementing AP domains is provided in the SDTM Implementation Guide: Associated Persons (SDTMIG-AP)' — omits URL and the specific enumeration 'rules, advice, and examples'. Semantic core (SDTMIG-AP provides implementation guidance) is preserved.

PDF verbatim: 'Unless an exception is described in the SDTMIG-AP, all other general assumptions about SDTM and SDTMIG variables and domains apply to AP data.' No MD atom contains this conditional rule about AP data inheriting general SDTM/SDTMIG assumptions unless SDTMIG-AP specifies an exception.

PDF verbatim: 'The variables in the following table are used in AP domains.' MD verbatim (PARAGRAPH_INVALID_AGENT_ERROR): 'The table of variables used in Associated Persons Data includes all variables from the general observation classes, with the addition of AP-specific identifiers:' — MD adds the characterization 'all variables from the general observation classes, with the addition of AP-specific identifiers' not stated in the PDF sentence. Core intro-to-table function is preserved.

PDF verbatim: 'APID is always included in each record in an AP domain.' No MD atom states that APID is always included in each record. md_model_04_a007 discusses APID's identifier role but not the always-included-per-record rule.

PDF verbatim: 'If the associated person is related to a study subject, RSUBJID identifies that subject; if the individual is related to a study device, RDEVID identifies the device.' MD verbatim (within md_model_04_a008 linking variables bullet) covers RSUBJID and RDEVID separately with equivalent definitions. Core identification logic preserved.

PDF verbatim: 'SREL describes the relationship of the associated person to a study subject, a study device, or the study.' MD verbatim (within md_model_04_a008): '**SREL** — Subject, Device, or Study Relationship: if RSUBJID is populated, describes the relationship of the associated person(s) to the subject or pool identified in RSUBJID. If RDEVID is populated, describes the relationship to the device identified in RDEVID. If RSUBJID and RDEVID are null, SREL describes the relationship to the study identified in STUDYID.' MD expands the three conditions explicitly; PDF summarizes. Semantic content is preserved.

PDF verbatim: 'Associated Persons—Additional Identifier Variables' (heading level 2). MD verbatim: '### Variables Used in Associated Persons Data' (heading level 3). Different wording: PDF specifies 'Additional Identifier Variables' while MD says 'Variables Used in Associated Persons Data'. Both introduce the AP variables table section.

PDF TABLE_HEADER verbatim has 12 columns: '# | Variable Name | Variable Label | Type | Format | Role | Variable(s) Qualified | Usage Restrictions | Variable C-code | Definition | Notes | Examples'. MD TABLE_HEADER verbatim has 5 columns: '| Variable | Label | Type | Role | Notes |'. MD retains informational columns (Variable, Label, Type, Role, Notes) but drops Format, Variable(s) Qualified, Usage Restrictions, Variable C-code, Examples, and the row-number (#) column.

PDF TABLE_ROW verbatim has 12-column structure including row number '1', Format (empty), Variable(s) Qualified (empty), Usage Restrictions (empty), Variable C-code (empty), Examples (empty). MD TABLE_ROW verbatim has 5 columns dropping all empty/metadata columns. Definition text 'Identifier for a single associated person, a group of associated persons, or a pool...' is preserved verbatim in MD Notes column.

PDF TABLE_ROW verbatim includes: 'RSUBJID may be populated with the USUBJID of the related subject or the POOLID of the related pool. RSUBJID will be null for data about associated persons who are related to the study but not to any study subjects.' MD TABLE_ROW verbatim includes the USUBJID/POOLID sentence but omits the null condition sentence: 'RSUBJID will be null for data about associated persons who are related to the study but not to any study subjects.' Semantic overlap ~0.75 (null condition missing from MD).

PDF TABLE_ROW verbatim has 12-column structure with empty metadata columns (Format, Variable(s) Qualified, Usage Restrictions, Variable C-code, Examples). MD TABLE_ROW verbatim has 5 columns retaining Variable, Label, Type, Role, and definition text 'Identifier for a related device. RDEVID will be populated with the SPDEVID of the related device.' which matches PDF Definition column verbatim. Only empty/metadata columns dropped.

PDF TABLE_ROW verbatim for SREL includes full three-condition definition explicitly referencing 'APID', 'RSUBJID', 'RDEVID', 'STUDYID' by name in each condition branch. MD TABLE_ROW verbatim simplifies: 'If RSUBJID is populated, describes the relationship to the subject or pool. If RDEVID is populated, describes the relationship to the device. If both are null, describes the relationship to the study.' MD omits explicit APID and STUDYID references in the conditions, retains the three-condition structure. Informational columns dropped (empty metadata columns from PDF 12-column format).

PDF verbatim: 'Relationships of an associated person to study subjects may be represented in the Associated Persons Relationships dataset.' This sentence appears verbatim as the first sentence in md_model_04_a021 (PARAGRAPH_INVALID_AGENT_ERROR merging two sentences). No semantic difference.

PDF verbatim: 'When an individual has multiple collected relationships to a study subject or study subjects, those multiple relationships must be represented in that relationships dataset.' This sentence appears verbatim as the second sentence in md_model_04_a021 (PARAGRAPH_INVALID_AGENT_ERROR merging two sentences). Keyword 'must' present in both PDF source verbatim and MD verbatim. No semantic difference.
